items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed, 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street will not be heard on the original date and will receive a new hearing notice when scheduled.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th, indicating ongoing work to improve traffic safety and coordination at this major corridor intersection. Residents using Bascom Avenue can expect continued construction and planning activity.

items.headline: Active Transportation Plan update: Phase 2 progress on biking and walking
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised scoring criteria and next steps. This plan shapes how the city invests in pedestrian and bicycle infrastructure going forward.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract for unnamed infrastructure project
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution to approve project plans and award a construction contract, though the agenda title does not specify which project or infrastructure improvement is being funded. Residents may want to review the full meeting minutes to determine if this affects their neighborhood.

items.headline: Milpitas seeks federal housing and community development funds
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which governs how federal housing and community development dollars are spent in Milpitas. This could signal new affordable housing initiatives or neighborhood improvements in the planning stages.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved near downtown
items.summary: Trillium is building a 65-unit senior apartment complex at 678 E St John St for $8.8 million, addressing San José's growing need for affordable senior housing. This is one of the larger residential projects recently approved in the city.

items.headline: City Council approves emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: San José City Council approved a new Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1st, which will affect how and where temporary housing for unhoused residents can be located throughout the city. This policy change could influence homelessness response efforts and neighborhood impacts across San José.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit over Mary Avenue safety continues
items.summary: The city is in ongoing litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. The case (26CV488611) is being heard in Santa Clara Superior Court.
